title: Resume/CL generation refactor
description: Swap LaTeX for Typst (match RAS pipeline). User-agnostic templates driven by profile.yaml. Remove all Josh-specific content from tailor.py and templates.

title: Deterministic scoring engine
description: Port RAS scoring framework into code. Config-driven weighted scoring: role type, domain, company tier, location, compensation. No LLM. Score threshold gates Claude calls. Start threshold conservatively low, log both code-score and Claude-score to calibrate.

title: Connection cache with age-based refresh
description: Import LinkedIn connections from export or RAS extract_linkedin_connections.py output. Local lookup: do I know anyone at {company}? Age-based refresh policy. Enrich Discord alerts with connection info.

title: Dismiss with reason (not hard delete)
description: All 'x' actions (Discord and dashboard) should dismiss with a reason, not hard delete. Dismissed jobs need to stay in the DB (marked as rejected with a reason) so: 1) we learn from dismissal patterns for future filtering, 2) the scraper doesn't re-ingest them as new. Dashboard X should prompt for a reason (like Discord does), set status=rejected, and store the reason. Discord dismiss already does this. Unify the behavior.

title: Web UI + DB personalization
description: Remove hardcoded profile UUID from Supabase schema. System prompt reads from profile.yaml or DB. Remove Josh-specific seed data. Make web UI fully config-driven.

title: Detect reposted roles with modified URLs
description: LinkedIn sometimes reposts the same role with a different URL (new job ID). Detect these by matching on title+company combinations, or by fuzzy-matching descriptions. When detected, link to the original job record rather than creating a new one. This prevents duplicate kanban cards for the same role.

title: Config refactor epic
description: Externalize all user-specific config into yaml files. Make codebase user-agnostic. LLM drafts initial config from resume, then deterministic code handles filtering/scoring.

title: LinkedIn post monitoring for hiring signals
description: Add a scraping tier that monitors LinkedIn posts (not just job listings) for hiring signals — keywords like 'hiring', 'opening', 'looking for', 'join my team', 'we need', etc. Posts from connections and company pages are high-signal and low-competition (no formal applicant count). Could use authenticated session since volume is low. Complements the JobSpy discovery tier.

title: Claude relevance as calibrated fallback
description: Claude only fires for jobs above deterministic threshold. Prompt templates from config/prompts/relevance.yaml, not hardcoded. Log both code-score and Claude-score. Track false negative rate to gradually tighten threshold.

title: Deterministic filter pipeline
description: Replace Claude relevance check with deterministic filters for: experience-years regex extraction, location string matching, industry/title keyword matching from filters.yaml, salary floor from config. Only edge cases go to Claude.

title: Commute-aware location filtering
description: Replace 3-city parallel queries with single radius search from Orinda (~40mi). Add deterministic commute filter: precomputed commute times from Piedmont for known cities, lat/lng estimation based on nearest precomputed city (not Piedmont). Shuttle adjustments make commute time usable for work, not just shorter. Weekly commute time estimate: assume 3 days/week for hybrid unless posting is explicit about in-office days. Config-driven: known_cities, shuttle_companies, max_weekly_commute_hours.

title: Config schema + LLM drafting CLI
description: Define profile.yaml, search.yaml, scoring.yaml, filters.yaml schemas. Build CLI: python setup_config.py --resume path/to/resume. LLM reads resume, drafts all 4 configs, user reviews/edits. Port scoring framework from RAS job_factor_lookups.xlsx.

title: Scraper schedule + saved-jobs
description: Configurable schedule from schedule.yaml: 15min daytime (6am-8pm PT weekdays), 60min overnight/weekends. Add saved-jobs periodic check (less frequent). Cron generation from config.
